title: General characteristic chemical properties of the first set of transition elements, titanium to copper
summary: 9701 A Level — complex ions, ligand substitution, and redox chemistry of transition metals.
learningObjectives: Describe formation of complex ions with ligands.
Write redox equations for common transition metal ions.
Relate properties to d-block electronic structure.

simpleExplanation.title: Complexes and redox
simpleExplanation.summary: Transition metals form complex ions with ligands. Many act as oxidising agents with variable oxidation states.
steps: Ligand: lone pair donor — H₂O, NH₃, Cl⁻.
Substitution: colour change shows new complex.
MnO₄⁻, Cr₂O₇²⁻ — strong oxidisers in acid.

sections.content: **Transition metal chemistry** (28.2) builds on **28.1**. Show **ligand exchange** equations and **colour changes**.

sections.label: Mark a transition metal chemistry question
